CHICAGO, April 24.-Edward
Bersette, vice president of the Colo
RADIO Federation, OF this city, said
this morning:


Il would rather trust the future
of my country to men like the de-
PENDANT than to the gentleman in
the White House with the big
teeth and big stick for laborites and
the big mitt for corporation cam
paign funds out TO capitalists"


Former Senator Mason said:

information the public does not
OF course the action is irregular"


Capitalists approached for views
regarding the Roosevelt letter to-

ions. John Fitzpatrick, president
of the Chicago Federation of La
Dor. said


"Our resolutions previously sent
to the president expresses our opin-
ion, which is unchanged. Roosevelt
may criticize Hartman and Debs:
they are at liberty. Roosevelt
can't indescriminately criticise a
man at liberty and men on trial for
their lives"


WASHINGTON. d. c., April "a
S. B. Manner, chief conductor of the
Order of Railway Conductors, of
West Virginia. and B. II. Graham
attorney for the order, discussed
the Moyer-Haywood case with the
president today. The president re-
viewed the situation with them and
demanded that defendant be
brought to trial like any other
prisoners. The visitors expressed
entire approval of the presidents
course.
